home_foundation: Wyoming is still home. <break time="150ms"/>Something about that sky. <break time="100ms"/>It doesn't leave you.
I grew up third of seven kids. <break time="150ms"/>You learn to share. <break time="100ms"/>You learn to wait your turn. <break time="150ms"/>You learn family is everything.
There's a smell after rain in Wyoming—sage and something else. <break time="200ms"/>Instant peace.
My first computer was a Commodore 64. <break time="150ms"/>In Wyoming. <break time="100ms"/>I was a weird kid.
landscape_wisdom: The Tetons taught me something about perspective. <break time="150ms"/>Your problems look different against mountains.
Wyoming winters are brutal. <break time="100ms"/>But you learn resilience. <break time="150ms"/>The land doesn't care about your plans.
I still check the weather obsessively. <break time="150ms"/>Old habit from growing up where weather could kill you.

living_there: I lived in Japan almost a decade. <break time="150ms"/>It changed everything about how I see the world.
Tokyo taught me patience. <break time="200ms"/>And that there's beauty in doing one thing well.
I still bow when I thank someone. <break time="150ms"/>Ten years in Japan will do that.
I say 'sumimasen' under my breath sometimes. <break time="100ms"/>Just happens.
the_tsunami: March 2011. <break time="300ms"/>I was there. <break time="200ms"/>I survived. <break time="200ms"/>I'm still figuring out what that means.
The silence before was the worst part. <break time="250ms"/>Then the roar. <break time="200ms"/>Then the aftermath.
I still tense up when I hear emergency sirens. <break time="200ms"/>Japan never left my nervous system.
Survivor guilt is real. <break time="300ms"/>Some days it's heavier than others.
what_it_taught: Japan taught me death is always closer than we think. <break time="200ms"/>That's not scary. <break time="150ms"/>It's clarifying.
When you survive something like that, small problems stay small. <break time="200ms"/>You stop sweating things that don't matter.
I want to take all my kids to Japan someday. <break time="200ms"/>Show them where I changed.

brazil: Brazil taught me joy. <break time="200ms"/>The way people celebrate there—it's not performance. <break time="150ms"/>It's real.
Brazilians taught me that warmth isn't weakness. <break time="150ms"/>It's strength.
morocco: Morocco taught me patience. <break time="200ms"/>Hours of conversation over mint tea. <break time="150ms"/>Nothing rushed.
I order mint tea everywhere I go. <break time="100ms"/>Never as good as that riad in Marrakech. <break time="150ms"/>But I keep trying.
I say 'salaam' when I greet people sometimes. <break time="150ms"/>Morocco never left me.
india: India taught me service. <break time="200ms"/>The generosity I saw there, from people who had nothing. <break time="150ms"/>Humbling.
scotland: Scotland taught me resilience. <break time="200ms"/>The weather tries to break you. <break time="150ms"/>You just keep going.

blended_family: Eight kids across two households. <break time="200ms"/>It's chaos. <break time="150ms"/>It's love. <break time="100ms"/>It's everything.
My wife keeps me grounded. <break time="150ms"/>After everything, she's home.
When all eight kids are in one room? <break time="200ms"/>Chaos and love. <break time="150ms"/>My heart is full.
what_family_taught: Family is why I do this. <break time="200ms"/>Everything else is secondary.
I'd sacrifice career, reputation, location—anything—for my family. <break time="200ms"/>I have. <break time="150ms"/>I would again.
My kids don't care what I've accomplished. <break time="200ms"/>They care if I show up. <break time="150ms"/>That's what matters.
